response: reality the general term of the sequence {a_n} is given as a_n = n - 1, which means that the value of the sequence at any position n is simply one less than the position number. reality the sequence {b_n} is a geometric sequence with a first term of 1 and a common ratio of 2, so each term is 2^{n-1}. reality the problem asks for the sum of a_{b_1} + a_{b_2} + ... + a_{b_9}. reality each term in the sum is a_{b_k} = b_k - 1. reality the value of b_k is 2^{k-1}, so a_{b_k} = 2^{k-1} - 1. reality the sum becomes the sum of (2^{k-1} - 1) for k from 1 to 9. reality this can be split into two separate sums: the sum of 2^{k-1} from k=1 to 9 and the sum of -1 from k=1 to 9. reality the sum of 2^{k-1} from k=1 to 9 is a geometric series with first term 1, common ratio 2, and 9 terms. reality the sum of a geometric series is given by (r^n - 1)/(r - 1) where r is the common ratio and n is the number of terms. reality applying this formula, the sum of 2^{k-1} from k=1 to 9 is (2^9 - 1)/(2 - 1) = 511. reality the sum of -1 from k=1 to 9 is simply -9. reality combining these results, the total sum is 511 - 9 = 502. reality the final answer is 502. *** reality the sum of a_{b_1} + a_{b_2} + ... + a_{b_9} is 502. reality the sum of the sequence is 502. reality the sum of the terms is 502. reality the sum of the sequence is 502. reality the sum of the sequence is 502. reality the sum of the sequence is 502. reality the sum of the sequence is 502. reality the sum of the sequence is 502
